headline: Users appreciate organization and gamification but report bugs, UI complexity, and premium pricing issues.
key_findings: Many users find the free version feature-rich, but others feel it's too limited (e.g., only 5 habits, no reminders).
Frequent bugs: widget not updating, reminder failures, saving loop, wrong holiday dates.
UI is considered cluttered and complicated, leading to hidden tasks and frustration.
Premium paywall is a major pain point: previously free features (trees) are locked, and premium is too expensive.
Missing features: history for medication tracking, hide completed habits, Arabic language support, web version, more customization.
Positive feedback: helps with routine, gamification is engaging, good customization.
action_items: Fix critical bugs: widget date rollover, reminders in power saving mode, saving loop, and holiday dates.
Rethink premium pricing: consider lower price points, PPP adjustments, or ad-supported free features.
Simplify UI by reducing clutter, ensuring tasks are visible, and improving onboarding.
Add requested features: history view for medication tracking, hide completed habits, web version, Arabic language support.
Restore previously free features or clearly communicate premium vs free limitations.
